reasoning: Q1 iteration 0 has critical architectural failure: 14,799 ms (308× slower) due to CSV text parsing instead of GenDB binary columnar storage. Two critical fixes identified: (1) Replace hardcoded CSV path with mmap-based columnar storage access—foundational for all optimizations, (2) Fix floating-point precision errors (acceptable rounding, low-impact). Per priority rules, critical fixes are mandatory and take precedence over performance optimizations.
focus_areas: Replace CSV text parsing with mmap-based binary columnar storage access
Load only required columns (l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus)
Fix floating-point precision in aggregate summation (use consistent order or Kahan summation)
strategy_notes: Iteration 1 focuses exclusively on critical fixes. The semantic/architecture issue (CSV parsing) is the root cause of 308× slowdown and must be fixed first. Once mmap-based columnar access is in place, subsequent iterations (2-3) can layer in performance optimizations: array-based aggregation, parallel morsel-driven scan, zone map skipping, and column pruning. This sequential approach ensures foundation is solid before optimization.
notes: Do not attempt performance optimizations in this iteration—critical fixes only. After iteration 1 succeeds, iteration 2 can target array-based aggregation (5-10× improvement) and iteration 3 can address parallelism (50-60× speedup on 64 cores). Remaining budget: 2 iterations.
